text.title: Voice Agent Rules
text.summary: VAD recording, history truncation, TTS response length, interrupt handling, and latency optimization rules.

rule.statement: Use VAD-based recording (webrtcvad or Silero VAD) instead of fixed-duration recording in any production deployment.
rule.rationale: Fixed-duration recording truncates variable-length speech and records silence for short utterances. VAD-based recording stops when the speaker stops.

example.code:
import webrtcvad, sounddevice as sd, collections
def record_until_silence(sample_rate: int = 16000, silence_ms: int = 1000) -> bytes: vad = webrtcvad.Vad(2) # aggressiveness 0-3 frame_ms, frame_bytes = 30, sample_rate * 30 // 1000 * 2 silence_frames = silence_ms // 30 ring = collections.deque(maxlen=silence_frames) frames = [] with sd.RawInputStream(samplerate=sample_rate, channels=1, dtype='int16') as s: while True: data, _ = s.read(frame_bytes // 2) frame = bytes(data) is_speech = vad.is_speech(frame, sample_rate) ring.append(is_speech) frames.append(frame) if len(frames) > silence_frames and not any(ring): break return b"".join(frames)

rule.statement: Keep TTS responses under 50 words per turn. Use tts-1 (not tts-1-hd) for real-time conversation.
rule.rationale: Longer responses feel unnatural and cannot be interrupted. tts-1-hd latency is not suitable for real-time — use it for pre-generated audio only.

rule.statement: Truncate conversation_history to the last 10 turns to prevent context overflow. Summarize older context into the system prompt.
rule.rationale: Conversation history grows unboundedly — will hit context limits after 20-50 turns on GPT-4o, causing silent truncation or API errors.

rule.statement: Always specify the language parameter explicitly in Whisper calls (e.g., language="uk", language="en").
rule.rationale: Auto-detection adds ~200ms latency and occasionally misidentifies similar languages (Ukrainian/Russian, Serbian/Croatian).

rule.statement: Use OpenAI Realtime API (WebSocket) for lowest-latency deployments — it eliminates the STT + LLM + TTS round-trip chain.
rule.rationale: The Realtime API delivers approximately 200ms end-to-end vs 650-1900ms for the chained approach.

rule.statement: Use client.audio.speech.with_streaming_response to stream TTS audio to the speaker as chunks arrive rather than buffering the full response.
rule.rationale: Streaming reduces perceived TTS latency from full-response time to first-byte time — typically 100-200ms faster.

antipattern.description: Implementing interrupt detection only as an afterthought.
antipattern.reason: If new audio is detected during TTS playback and the agent does not stop playback immediately, the user hears overlapping speech — a blocking UX failure.
